tasks.title: Make parallel loop avoid queue.json merge conflicts without losing task state
evidence: User request: "When using 'ralph run loop --parallel 2' there are ALWAYS merge conflicts due to the queue.json changes."
User request: "We need to make sure we don't wipe out the queue and we properly mark tasks complete and move the tasks to the done.json file too."
Repo fact: `.ralph/queue.json` is the source of truth for active work and `.ralph/done.json` is the archive of completed tasks.
plan: Reproduce the issue by running `ralph run loop --parallel 2` on a small queue and observe how/when `.ralph/queue.json` is modified across workspaces/branches.
Trace the parallel run code paths that mutate queue state and identify the exact cause of persistent git merge conflicts (write location, timing, ordering, and concurrent edits).

Design a conflict-free workflow that preserves the single-worker semantics: ensure only one place is responsible for mutating `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` and that workers cannot overwrite or drop tasks.
Implement the chosen approach (e.g., coordinator applies queued, per-task completion deltas to the canonical queue with atomic updates and deterministic ordering).

Add unit + integration tests that cover: successful completion, failures/retries, partial progress, concurrent worker finishes, and that `queue.json` is never wiped and completed tasks reliably move to `done.json`.
Update CLI help/docs as needed to document parallel queue semantics and any behavior changes; ensure parity with non-parallel `ralph run loop`.
Run `make ci` and `ralph queue validate` to confirm correctness and prevent regressions.
tasks.request: When using 'ralph run loop --parallel 2' there are ALWAYS merge conflicts due to the queue.json changes. We need to figure out a way to handle this to avoid merge conflicts. We need to make sure we don't wipe out the queue and we properly mark tasks complete and move the tasks to the done.json file too. We need to be able to reliably merge and mimic the currently reliable workflow of 'ralph run loop' when it runs without parallel workers.

tasks.title: Fix parallel state.json to auto-correct and auto-populate git branch
evidence: User request: 'Fix the state.json file to auto-correct and auto-populate and set the git branch properly. User previously ran a parallel run on branch '02022026' but has since changed to 'main' and this fails unless user manually clears .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json.'
Error: 'Parallel state base branch '02022026' does not match current branch 'main'.'
plan: Read the parallel state.rs implementation to understand how state.json is loaded and validated.
Identify where the base branch mismatch error is raised and how state.json is populated.
Implement auto-correction logic: when state.json exists but base_branch does not match current git branch, either update the state or clear it and regenerate.
Ensure state.json is auto-populated with correct current git branch when missing or stale.
Add tests covering branch mismatch auto-correction and auto-population scenarios.
Run make ci to verify changes.
tasks.request: Fix the state.json file to auto-correct and auto-populate and set the git branch properly. User previously ran a parallel run on branch '02022026' but has since changed to 'main' and this fails unless user manually clears .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json.

tasks.title: Prevent parallel loop from re-running tasks when no PR record exists
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:71-88 excludes only in-flight tasks and open PRs; completed tasks without PRs are eligible again.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:352-441 only records PRs when auto_pr/draft_on_failure is enabled; tasks without PRs are removed from tasks_in_flight and then become selectable again.
docs/configuration.md:236-238 notes auto_pr can be false (PR creation skipped), which currently allows repeated selection of the same task in parallel mode.
plan: Decide the expected behavior when auto_pr is false or draft_on_failure is false (e.g., treat the task as completed, or block re-selection until user intervention).
Implement a durable state marker (e.g., completed_without_pr list in parallel state) and exclude those task IDs during selection, or enforce auto_pr for parallel runs.
Add tests covering auto_pr=false and draft_on_failure=false to ensure tasks are not re-selected indefinitely; update docs to describe the finalized behavior.

tasks.title: Ensure workspace cleanup when parallel worker spawn fails
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:294-326 creates/syncs workspace before spawning worker; if spawn_worker fails, the workspace is not registered with the cleanup guard and is leaked.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:303-309 returns early on spawn failure, skipping cleanup for the untracked workspace.
plan: Register the workspace with the cleanup guard immediately after creation (before spawn) so any error path cleans it up.
Add a targeted test that simulates spawn failure and asserts the workspace is removed/cleaned by the guard.
Verify parallel cleanup tests still pass and add documentation if cleanup semantics change.
